text:
Global semiconductor equipment sales, the lithography, metrology, and photonics tools that build every chip, are projected to hit a record $133 billion in 2025 and $156 billion by 2027. 

Every AI breakthrough traces back to a fab, and every fab traces back to the equipment that builds it, which is exactly where this spending lands. 

Source: SEMI projections, December 2025

text:
翻译

$SIVE(Sivers Semiconductors)是我心目中最具吸引力的 CPO / 光子学(photonics)敞口标的。

回应一下虚假信息:我一股都没卖,也不打算卖任何一股。

我确实认为这家公司有一天会从现在约 21 亿美元市值,成长为下一个 800 亿美元以上的 $LITE(Lumentum)。

而且我个人有计划继续增持,并支持他们的并购(M&A)前景。

我相信财报电话会议纪要(earnings transcripts)将非常正面。

因为在过去几个月里,我们已经陆续发现以下信息:

AlChip(世芯)/ Amazon(亚马逊)的定向增发,对 Ayar Labs 是利好 → 对 $SIVE 而言意味着被设计进 Trainium 4(亚马逊第四代自研 AI 芯片)。

纬颖(Wiwynn)+ Ayar 的 CPO 放量。

$JBL(捷普 / Jabil)1.6T 光收发器爬坡,Sivers 的进展比市场预期更快(具备相对显著的护城河 + 有多少产能客户就要多少需求)。

O-Net(昂纳科技)与 $SIVE 一起扩大 ELS(外部光源)工作规模。
$YSS(York Space Systems)收购了 $SIVE 的 Allspace 主要合作伙伴,正把 Sivers 设计进太空国防主承包商(Space defense primes)。

$SIVE 获得新的 CHIPS 法案(CHIPS ACT)资助。

$POET(POET Technologies)下半年放量,以及他们新拿到的从 5,000 万美元上调到 5 亿美元的订单(以 $SIVE 作为光源)。

信息发现:$AAPL(苹果)正在为下一代消费电子设备使用 $SIVE 激光器。
信息发现:与 Lightelligence(已上市,市值 100 亿美元以上)+ Lightmatter 的关联,二者可能是客户。

Celestial(Celestial AI)放量,$MRVL(美满电子 / Marvell)给出指引信号。

新客户正在与 $SIVE 合作 TFLN(薄膜铌酸锂)方案,例如 Lightium。

$AMD(超微)选择 $GFS(格芯)做 CPO,而 GFS 将 Sivers 列为两家激光供应商之一。

Ayar 从官网上撤下了 $MTSI(MACOM)/ $LITE(Lumentum),并把 $SIVE 标示为主要供应商 / 独家供应源。

Ayar 为放量爬坡融资 5 亿美元(英特尔、联发科、英伟达、AMD 等参与)。
2025 年年报释出可插拔(pluggable)TAM(总潜在市场)扩张的信号。

预计很快在纳斯达克(Nasdaq)上市。

被纳入 MSCI 小盘指数 / Nasdaq OMX 指数,使贝莱德(BlackRock)、先锋(Vanguard)等成为被动买入者。
2025 年年报释出并购信号 + 两位拥有该领域经验的新董事会成员加入。

$NOK(诺基亚)在 2025 年年报中显示为可能的客户。
$LITE 因 EML 合同导致 CW 产能瓶颈,而 $SIVE 释出已与稳懋(WIN Semiconductor)达成产能协议的信号,使其有望成为 CPO 板块的瓶颈所有者 + 咽喉控制点(chokepoint)。

以上所有市场研究都是在财报发布之前完成的。

任何财报结果都只是对已完成的供应链映射的确认而已。

我认为没人会在乎上一季度的营收数字,因为 $SIVE 是一只极具吸引力的、押注 2027 年(尤其是下半年起)的多头标的。

我现在唯一关注的事情有:

整体光子学超级周期(photonics supercycle)的 TAM 扩张(例如光学引擎、ELS、可插拔模块),无论是来自并购还是技术发展。

现有公司的放量爬坡预期。

纳斯达克上市时间表,以便为公司的并购努力提供更多流动性支撑。
任何关于 CPO / 可插拔领域释出的新客户信号。
embedded_text:
$SIVE is the most compelling CPO/photonics exposure to me.

Addressing the disinformation: I haven’t sold and don’t plan to sell a single share. 

I do think this ends up the next $80B+ $LITE one day from ~$2.1B.

And I personally have plans to acquire more ownership + support https://t.co/Mb5eRuL2N7

text:
Been doing a lot of reading on the supply chain this year. I am not always right. But the ones that were right, were very right.

$IQE: 17p → 48 (only independent epiwafer foundry globally, photonics supply chain bottleneck)

$SOI: €70 → €192 (silicon photonics substrate near monopoly for AI data centers)

$SIVE: SEK 30 → SEK 70 (CPO laser source, InP chokepoint in the AI optical stack)

$ENSI: 72p → 116p (ASIC design house, AI data centers + Space + Post-Quantum)

$LBIRD: €19→ €26.5 (European defense laser leader, NATO rearmament cycle)

$CIFR: $15 → $25 (Bitcoin miner transitioning to AI data center operator, $9.3B contracted)

$ONDS: $8 → $13.6 (autonomous drone prime contractor, +1,065% Q1 YoY revenue)

$LPK: €18 → €24 (only proven glass substrate LIDE process globally, AI packaging)

None of these were obvious at entry. All had real risks. Most were ignored.

The market repricing confirms the reading.

text:
Looks like we agree on the similarities between $CPSH and $SIVE when it was just starting to break out...

Completely different businesses, but both are phenomenal chokepoint plays.

Back when $SIVE was sitting at ~8 SEK, most people saw it as just another small-cap photonics name.

Little did they know, it was hugely undervalued as a supplier sitting at a genuine bottleneck in the optical interconnect space... exactly the layer AI needed as data movement became the constraint.

The market eventually caught on and it proceeded to ~10x.

$CPSH feels like it's at that same pre-recognition stage, sitting at the same valuation as $SIVE before it took off.

> $SIVE: photonics/optical interconnects for AI datacenters

> $CPSH: AlSiC thermal management for defense, space, and potentially AI chips

Both are critical suppliers that get overlooked until market demand forces the re-rating.

And remember, $CPSH is the only meaningful US-based vertically integrated AlSiC supplier, with sole-source status on many applications per their own 10-K...

When the market starts to recognize this as a true bottleneck, it'll re-rate hard and fast.

$SIVE already showed how that plays out.

text:
AI capex spend is expected to go to "$3 to $4 trillion annually" by 2030 from $NVDA Jensen Huang projections. 

You're not bullish enough. 

And it might be a good idea to stay exposed + own the keys of the AI Kingdom:

-> $AXTI controls the materials buildout with photonics.
-> $SOI controls the AI buildout with silicon photonics.
-> $SIVE controls laser chokepoints for CPO.
-> $IQE controls Western epiwafer supply chains for photonics. 

All these started off as tiny companies, yet the trillions of projected capex gradually upward to them. 

There's many more in other industries as well. 

-> AI Capex flows to Neoclouds like $NBIS. 
-> AI Capex flows to memory like $MU and $SNDK. 

And many of the "commodity" materials or "science projects" for the past 20 years now a sudden shift in exponential TAM expansion.  

We're witnessing the next industrial revolution with Artificial Intelligence + Physical AI.

text: My point is not that photonics as a theme is not interesting, I have invested in all US companies $AAOI $LITE $COHR even a small player like $OPTX but my point is that $SIVE is a small Swedish firm with no experience or ability to deliver on large orders without rapid expansion. This will be extreamly hard and even if performed successfully the company is overvalued at $2.5B. You can’t compare a small photonics company from Sweden with other large US firms that have had high valuations for yeafs (like Nvidia or Tesla or Palantir) these large companies have huuuge moats - $SIVE barely have the manufacturing capabilities neccessary at its current state.

text:
🚨 A new ETF just filed that’s a pure bet on the photonics buildout: $LYTE

📈Roundhill - Same ETF shop that ran $DRAM to $1B AUM at fastest clip. Now- Photonics & Optics ETF. Holdings not disclosed yet.

But the prospectus tells you exactly what’s getting in — if you read the fine print, let me break it down for you🧵

Here’s the part that matters : there’s one hard rule in the filing:
⛔️A company needs ≥50% of revenue from photonics/optics to qualify.
That single number tells you who’s in and who’s out. 👇

✅ CLEARS the 50% bar (the pure-plays):
$LITE — optical transceivers, 1060nm VCSELs
$COHR — lasers + optical components
$AAOI — datacenter optics
$POET — silicon photonics interposer
$IPGP — industrial fiber lasers
$CIEN — optical interconnect systems
$CRDO — AI interconnect
$SIVEF — InP lasers & Optic components
These are the core of the fund.

The sleeper angle nobody’s talking about: 🇨🇳
The prospectus explicitly builds in Chinese exposure — China A-Shares + VIE structures.
It says outright: the fund “invests significantly in the securities of Asian issuers.”
A US-listed photonics ETF with deliberate mainland exposure. Unusual.

Why that matters?? 
Some of the most aggressive optical-component scaling is happening in Asia.
$LYTE isn’t ignoring it — it’s writing A-shares into the strategy doc.
That’s a geographic edge most US investors won’t have on day one

Bottom line:
$LYTE = the first pure-play photonics ETF
↳ 50% revenue screen = real pure-plays only
↳ Pure-plays in, conglomerates out
↳ Built-in Asia/China exposure 👇

text:
I agree with the direction here.

The market is probably still early in understanding how CPO and photonics will flow through the numbers. A lot of the real volume is still ahead of us.

Bullish photonics, but I’m not going all in. Conversion still has to happen, and not every company in the chain will be a winner.
